project_description: Convert an attached garage into a small home bakery with two employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation plans.

sources.title: Blacksburg Home Occupation Standards
sources.excerpt: Home occupation bakeries may be conditionally permitted in mixed-use districts when parking, signage, food safety, and fire review conditions are satisfied.

project_description: Convert part of a rural home into a small home bakery with two employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation plans.

sources.title: Montgomery County Home Occupation Zoning Guidance
sources.excerpt: Home occupations in unincorporated Montgomery County must remain secondary to the residence, avoid exterior impacts, and food-preparation businesses should contact the Health Department about food service permit requirements.

project_description: Operate a minor home occupation (small graphic design studio) from a single-family home with two part-time employees, posted business hours, and no exterior renovation or new construction.

sources.title: Sec. 42-93. - Permitted uses.
sources.excerpt: In the R-1 Single-Family Residential District, structures to be erected or land to be used shall be for one or more of the following uses: (1) Single-family dwellings, including a family and one unrelated individual per unit. ... (5) Minor home occupations. Major home occupations with a conditional use permit.

project_description: Open a small cafe with two employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation plans.
Open a small home bakery with two employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation plans.
Open a restaurant with six employees, posted operating hours, and tenant improvement construction.

sources.title: Residential Bakery Conditions
sources.excerpt: Home bakeries in residential districts require planning review and neighborhood impact screening.

project_description: Open a sit-down restaurant with an outdoor patio dining area in an existing commercial building in the B-3 General Business district, with posted operating hours, about twelve employees, and interior renovation only.

sources.title: Sec. 30-438.1. - Permitted principal and accessory uses.
sources.excerpt: The following uses of buildings and premises shall be permitted in the B-3 district ... (45) Restaurants, tearooms, cafes, delicatessens, ice cream parlors and similar food and beverage service establishments, including entertainment in conjunction therewith. Such establishments may include areas outside completely enclosed buildings.

project_description: Run a home-based tailoring business inside a single-family house in the R-1 district, worked solely by household residents with no outside employees, posted daytime operating hours, and no renovation or new construction.

sources.title: Sec. 30-402.2. - Permitted accessory uses and structures.
sources.excerpt: Accessory uses and structures, including the following, which are customarily incidental and clearly subordinate to permitted principal uses, shall be permitted in the R-1 district ... (2) Home occupations;

sources.title: Sec. 30-694.1. - Home occupation regulations.
sources.excerpt: The following provisions shall apply to home occupations in all districts in which they are permitted: (1) Employment. Only persons living together as a family on the premises shall be employed on the premises in the conduct of the home occupation. (2) Location. The home occupation shall be conducted within the dwelling unit or within a completely enclosed accessory building on the same property.

project_description: Open a small coffee shop with six employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation only in an existing downtown storefront.

sources.title: Chapter 5. USE REGULATIONS - 5.1.3 Permitted Principal Uses by Zoning District
sources.excerpt: Restaurants: Permitted by right in: PD, NC, CC, DD, 1ST, 5TH, RC4, RC6, RC12, LI. Permitted subject to additional use regulations under Subsection 5.1.4 (conditional) in: none. Not permitted in any district not listed for this use.

project_description: Run a home-based graphic design business inside a single-family house, worked solely by household residents with no outside employees, posted daytime operating hours, and no renovation or new construction.

sources.title: Chapter 5. USE REGULATIONS
sources.excerpt: 5.2 Accessory Uses and Structures. Accessory uses customarily incidental and clearly subordinate to permitted principal uses include Home Occupations, subject to the accessory use and structure standards of Chapter 5.

project_description: Open a sit-down restaurant with eight employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation only in an existing storefront on Battlefield Boulevard.

sources.title: § 7-602. - Table of permitted and conditional uses in business districts.
sources.excerpt: SECTION 7-602. TABLE OF PERMITTED AND CONDITIONAL USES IN BUSINESS DISTRICTS. 58 Eating and Drinking Places with Seating: Permitted (P) in B-1, B-2, B-5 and MXD/U in the rural, suburban and urban overlay areas. 58 Eating and Drinking Places Without Seating: Permitted (P) in B-1, B-2, B-5 and MXD/U. Restaurants and similar eating and drinking establishments with seating are permitted by right in the B-2 general business district.

project_description: Operate a home child day care service inside my single-family house with two employees, posted operating hours, and no exterior renovation.

sources.title: § 6-2102. - Table of permitted and conditional uses in residential districts.
sources.excerpt: SECTION 6-2102. TABLE OF PERMITTED AND CONDITIONAL USES IN RESIDENTIAL DISTRICTS. 835 Home Child Day Care Services: Permitted (P) in all residential districts. 835 Child Day Care Services Requiring State Licensing: allowed only through the granting of a conditional use permit (C) by city council in all residential districts.

project_description: Establish a farm winery with a small tasting room using grapes grown on my property, with four employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation only of the existing barn.

project_description: Open a sit-down restaurant with ten employees and posted operating hours in an existing commercial building, interior renovation only.

sources.title: Sec. 32-401.11. - Uses permitted by right.
sources.excerpt: The following uses shall be permitted by right in the B-1 District: 10. Child-care facility. 16. Craft brewery (not to exceed production of 10,000 barrels per year. May or may not be associated with a restaurant). 25. Hotel or motel. 38. Office. 51. Restaurant.

project_description: Establish a farm winery with a tasting room on my five-acre agricultural property, with four employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation only of the existing barn.

sources.title: Sec. 32-301.02. - Uses permitted by right.
sources.excerpt: The following uses shall be permitted by right in the A-1 district: 1. Except for the keeping of domestic fowl as regulated in Part 508, agricultural uses, the keeping of livestock, and fishery uses, farm wineries and breweries with limited brewery licenses in accordance with section 32-300.07.10, on lots two acres or greater. Accessory structures such as, but not limited to, barns, sheds, and stables shall be permitted as required for bona fide agricultural uses.

project_description: Open a sit-down restaurant with eight employees and posted operating hours in an existing commercial building, interior renovation only.

sources.title: Sec. 22.2.1 - By right.
sources.excerpt: The following uses shall be permitted in any C-1 district, subject to the applicable requirements of this chapter. a. The following retail sales and service establishments: 1. Antique, gift, jewelry, notion and craft shops. 4. Drug store, pharmacy. 6. Food and grocery stores including such specialty shops as bakery, candy, milk dispensary. c. ... 2. Convenience stores. 3. Restaurants. b. The following services and public establishments: 1. Offices. 13. Child day centers (reference 5.1.06).

project_description: Establish a farm winery with a tasting room on my rural property, with four employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation only of the existing barn.

sources.excerpt: The following uses shall be permitted by right in the RA district, subject to the applicable requirements of this chapter: 3. Agriculture, forestry, and fishery uses except as otherwise expressly provided. 17. Farm winery uses, events, and activities authorized by section 5.1.25(a),(b), and (c)(2).

project_description: Open a sit-down restaurant with eight employees and posted operating hours in an existing commercial building, interior renovation only.

sources.title: Sec. 8-1. - Use regulations.
sources.excerpt: Structures to be erected and land to be used shall be for the following uses: 8-1-9 Banks and financial institutions. 8-1-20 Bed and Breakfast Inns, Bed and Breakfast Homestays, Hotels and motels. 8-1-36 Restaurants. 8-1-37 Retail stores. 8-1-38 Schools.

project_description: Open a plant manufacturing and processing bakery goods with twelve employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation only of an existing industrial building.

sources.title: Sec. 11-1. - Use regulations.
sources.excerpt: Structures to be erected or land to be used shall be for the following uses: 11-1-1 The manufacture, compounding, processing, packing, or treatment of such products as bakery goods, candy, cosmetics, dairy products, drugs, perfumes, pharmaceuticals and high-purity chemicals, perfumed toilet soap, toiletries, and food products. 11-1-2 The manufacture, compounding, assembling, or treatment of articles or merchandise from prepared materials including wood, cloth, and plastics.

project_description: Open a business studio and office for a marketing firm with six employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation only of an existing commercial building.
normalized_address: 1152 Virginia Beach Blvd, Virginia Beach, VA 23451

sources.excerpt: (a) Principal and conditional uses. The following chart lists those uses permitted within the B-1 through B-4K Business Districts, as either principal uses indicated by a 'P' or as conditional uses indicated by a 'C.' Business studios, offices and clinics P P P P P P P. Day-care centers and child care education centers C C P P P P C. Drugstores, beauty shops and barbershops P P P P P P P.

project_description: Establish a farm winery with a vineyard and tasting room on my agricultural property, with four employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation only of the existing barn.

sources.title: Sec. 401. - Use regulations.
sources.excerpt: (a) Principal and conditional uses. The following chart lists those uses permitted within the AG-1 and AG-2 Agricultural Districts, as either principal uses indicated by a 'P' or as conditional uses indicated by a 'C.' Agricultural, aquacultural and horticultural operations, including orchards, vineyards, nurseries and the raising and grazing of livestock, poultry and swine and the keeping of bees P P. Farm wineries, farm breweries and farm distilleries, subject to the provisions of section 209.1 P P.

project_description: Open a small retail store selling home goods with four employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation only of an existing commercial building in the C1 Retail Commercial District.

sources.title: Sec. 45-2002. - Permitted uses.
sources.excerpt: (a) Principal uses. Principal uses permitted in this district are set forth in Article IV, section 45-402 (b) Accessory uses. Generally, uses accessory to principal uses allowed in this district are permitted. However, certain accessory uses may have special requirements or require a special exception. See Article V and Article XXXII to determine what special requirements may apply.

project_description: Run a home occupation providing freelance bookkeeping services inside my single-family home, with no employees, no posted public hours, and no exterior renovation, using less than 400 square feet of floor space, in the R4 Single-Family Dwelling District.

sources.title: Sec. 45-517. - Home occupation.
sources.excerpt: No home occupation shall be permitted that: (1) Makes use of more than four hundred (400) square feet of floor space. (2) Makes use of any accessory building. (3) Changes the outside appearance of the dwelling or is visible from the street or has any other exterior indication of the home occupation. (4) Displays or stores materials outside. (5) Creates a nuisance varying the residential character of the premises.

project_description: Build a new one-family detached dwelling with four employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation only, meeting the minimum 22,000 square foot lot area and 110-foot frontage in the R-22 One Family Residential District.

sources.title: Sec. 4-44. - Lot area.
sources.excerpt: All lots shall be a minimum of twenty-two thousand (22,000) square feet with a minimum frontage of one hundred ten (110) feet.

project_description: Build a light manufacturing and assembly facility with four employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation only of an existing building, meeting the 35-foot maximum building height in the M-1 Limited Manufacturing District.

sources.title: Sec. 7-4. - Height.
sources.excerpt: Buildings shall be a maximum of thirty-five (35) feet in height, unless otherwise approved by use permit in accordance with chapter 14.

project_description: Build a new one-family detached dwelling with four employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation only, meeting the minimum 25,000 square foot lot area and 50-foot minimum front yard setback in the R-1 One-Family Residence District.

sources.title: Sec. 24-3305. - R-1 One-Family Residence District.
sources.excerpt: R-1 District Dimensional Standards: Lot area, minimum 25,000 sf (dwellings); Lot width, minimum 150 feet; Structure height, maximum 40 feet (dwellings); Front yard, minimum 50 feet.

project_description: Build a light manufacturing and assembly facility with four employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation only of an existing building, entirely within an enclosed structure, meeting the 110-foot maximum structure height in the M-1 Light Industrial District.

sources.title: Sec. 24-3411. - M-1 Light Industrial District.
sources.excerpt: M-1 District Dimensional Standards: Structure height, maximum 110 feet; Front yard, minimum 25 feet; Interior side yard, minimum 0/15 feet; Rear yard, minimum 30 feet. Allowed uses include light manufacturing, laboratories, warehouses, and recycling, and many industrial activities are only allowed if conducted within enclosed buildings.

project_description: Build a new single-family detached dwelling with two employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation only, meeting the district's 15,000 square foot minimum lot area and 2 dwelling units per acre maximum density standard in the R-2 Residential District.

sources.title: 72-31.2: R-2 Residential District.
sources.excerpt: A. Purpose. The R-2 Residential District is established to provide for single-family detached dwellings in suburban-style subdivisions at a density not to exceed two dwelling units per acre. B. Dimensional standards. Residential Density, Maximum 2 dwelling units/acre; Lot Area, Minimum (square feet) 15,000.

project_description: Develop a mixed-use project with six employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation only, combining ground-floor retail and upper-story residential dwellings with two or more different use groups in the Planned Development-Mixed-Use (PD-MU) District.

sources.title: 72-33.3: Planned Development-Mixed-Use District.
sources.excerpt: E. Minimum use mix. (1) A PD-MU District shall include two or more different use groups from the following list: (a) Residential; (b) Lodging; (c) Office, Institutional, and Educational; (d) Retail and Consumer. (2) In a PD-MU District, no single use group shall make up more than 75% of the total gross floor area of the development.

project_description: Build a new single-family detached dwelling with two employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation only, meeting the district's 40-foot maximum height and 30-foot minimum front setback standards in the R-2 District.

sources.title: Sec. 35.2-42.4. - R-2 District development standards.
sources.excerpt: Exhibit IV-6: Summary of R-2 Development Standards Design Element Standards Cross-Reference Maximum height 40' 35.2-61.2 Minimum front setback (dimension A) 30' 35.2-61.3 Minimum side setback, interior (dimension B) 8' 35.2-61.3 Minimum side setback, exterior (dimension C) 30' 35.2-61.3 Minimum rear setback (dimension D) 30' 35.2-61.3 Minimum lot area (square feet) 10,000, with 5,000 per boarding unit 35.2-61.4 Maximum net density (dwelling units per acre/dwelling units per acre including accessory and boarding units) 4.35/8.7 35.2-61.4 Maximum lot coverage 20% 35.2-61.4

project_description: Develop a mixed-use building with six employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation only, combining ground-floor retail with upper-story multi-household residences in downtown Lynchburg's Planned Development-adjacent B-4 District, with no drive-in or drive-through service.

sources.title: Sec. 35.2-47.1. - B-4 District purpose.
sources.excerpt: The B-4 district is intended to: (a) Provide for a wide range of residential, commercial and other sales and service operations, serving the entire metropolitan area in downtown Lynchburg and other mixed use centers throughout the City; (b) Encourage a compatible mix of multi-household residences, business and related activities of relatively high densities and with adequate services where appropriate; (c) Assure public access to the James River for recreational purposes and to protect the aesthetic qualities of the riverfront.

project_description: Build a new single-family detached dwelling with two employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation only, meeting the district's 15,000 square foot minimum lot area and 35-foot minimum front setback standards in the R-1 Low Density, Single-Family Residential District.

sources.title: Sec. 130-262. - R-1 low density, single-family residential.
sources.excerpt: (a) Purpose and intent. The R-1 low density, single-family residential district is intended to preserve and promote quality residential-scale architecture and neighborhood character. This district is designed to provide for and encourage development of high-quality detached single-family homes at a low suburban density of approximately 2.9 dwelling units per acre or less. (b) Uses permitted. See section 130-241. (c) Minimum lot dimensions: (1) Area: 15,000 square feet. (2) Width: a. Interior lots, 100 feet at the front setback line. b. Corner lots, 110 feet at the front setback line. (d) Minimum setback requirements: (1) Front, all structures shall be located a minimum of 35 feet from the front lot line.

project_description: Develop a mixed-use project with six employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation only, combining ground-floor pedestrian-oriented retail with upper-story residential dwelling units in the B-3 City Center Commercial (Old Town) District.

sources.title: Sec. 130-303. - B-3 city center commercial (Old Town district).
sources.excerpt: (a) Purpose and intent. The B-3 city center commercial district is intended to implement the Comprehensive Plan's Downtown Character Area and to provide an attractive and successful mixed-use historic downtown with pedestrian-oriented retail, commercial, and institutional uses along with residential uses at urban densities that attract and serve both local and visitor populations. Recognizing the economic value of the existing historic downtown, it shall further be the intention of this district to encourage the retention and rehabilitation of structures and uses in the district that have historic and/or architectural significance.

project_description: Build a new single-family detached dwelling with two employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation only, in the RSF Residential Single Family District, consistent with the district's purpose of accommodating infill single-family development on Salem's relatively small residential lots.

sources.title: Sec. 106-202.1. - Statement of intent.
sources.excerpt: (A) The RSF Residential Single Family District is created in recognition that most residential neighborhoods in Salem are comprised primarily of single family dwellings on relatively small lots. It is anticipated that most future residential development will occur in this district. Proposed development standards such as lot sizes and frontage requirements in the RSF District reflect Salem's compact development pattern and recognition that future residential development will be "infill development" Limited uses other than residential are permitted.

project_description: Build a new single-family detached dwelling with two employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation only, in the Neighborhood Residential (NR) District, discouraging any use that would interfere with the district's quiet single-family character.

sources.title: Sec. 40.2-202. - Residential Zoning Districts.
sources.excerpt: (B) Neighborhood Residential (NR) District. The Neighborhood Residential (NR) district is established to accommodate single-family detached residential dwellings at low densities. District regulations are intended to discourage any use that substantially interferes with the development of single-family detached dwellings or that is detrimental to the quiet residential nature of the district.

project_description: Open a general office and retail sales building with six employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation only, in the HBD Highway Business District, consistent with the district's purpose of providing commercial and service uses along major transportation arterials.

sources.title: Sec. 106-214.1. - Statement of intent.
sources.excerpt: The HBD Highway Business District has been created to provide and allow a wide variety of commercial and service uses that are typically found along or near major transportation arterials. This district provides a location and development standards for more intensive commercial uses not appropriate for the Downtown (DBD) or Residential (RB) Districts.

project_description: Develop a mixed-use project with six employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation only, combining residential units with commercial and institutional uses in the General Mixed-Use (GMU) District.

sources.title: Sec. 40.2-203. - Commercial Zoning Districts.
sources.excerpt: (C) General Mixed-Use (GMU) District. The General Mixed-Use (GMU) district is established to provide a wide variety of professional offices and institutions and a broad range of light and moderate intensity commercial uses proximate to residential and more intense business districts so as to satisfy the demand for services. The district regulations are designed to encourage the formation and continuance of a compatible mixed-use environment that includes residential, commercial, and institutional uses.

project_description: Build a new single-family detached dwelling with two employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation only, in the R-2 low density residential district, consistent with the district's purpose of single-family residential development with low to moderate population densities.

sources.title: § 18.25.020: Permitted principal and accessory uses and structures.
sources.excerpt: Property and buildings in an R-2 district shall be used only for the following purposes: (1) Detached single-family dwellings, but not including trailer houses or mobile homes. (2) Temporary buildings for uses incidental to construction work, which buildings shall be immediately adjacent to said construction work and which buildings shall be removed upon completion or abandonment of the construction work. (3) Transportation and utility easements, alleys, and rights-of-way. (4) Accessory uses and buildings, provided such uses are incidental to the principal use and do not include any activity commonly conducted as a business.

project_description: Open a sit-down restaurant with two employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation only, in an existing storefront in downtown Staunton's B-2 general business district, which permits every B-1 local business use including eating and drinking establishments.

sources.title: § 18.60.020: Uses permitted.
sources.excerpt: The following uses shall be permitted in the B-2 general business district: (1) Any use permitted in the B-1 local business district. (2) Auto, manufactured home or truck parking, storage, service and repair shop, or sales lots; provided, that all manufactured homes are in a state of good repair and dismantled or junked cars or trucks unfit for operation on the highways shall not be stored therein. (3) Bakeries employing not more than 10 persons and where products are sold at retail on the premises.

sources.title: § 18.55.020: Uses permitted.
sources.excerpt: The following uses shall be permitted in the B-1 local business district: (1) Living and/or sleeping quarters shall be a permitted use when constructed above the ground and basement floors. (6) Barbershops, beauty parlors, chiropody, massages, or similar personal service shops. (7) Catering and delicatessen business. (11) Drugstore. (13) Eating and drinking establishments.

project_description: Build a new single-family detached dwelling with two employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation only, in the RM Residential Medium Density district, where Table 406-1 lists single-family detached dwellings as a permitted use.

project_description: Open a sit-down restaurant with two employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation only, in an existing storefront in the CBD Central Business District of downtown Suffolk, where Table 406-1 lists sit-down restaurants as a permitted use.

sources.title: SEC. 31-406. - USE REGULATIONS.
sources.excerpt: Those uses permitted as permitted uses, and conditional uses within each zoning district shall be those uses listed in the Use Matrices (Table 406-1) to this Ordinance. TABLE 406-1: PRINCIPAL USES PERMITTED IN ZONING DISTRICTS Specific Use Type Zoning Districts C A RR RE RL RLM RM RC RU RU-12 RU-18 RU-24 MUC-40 B-1 B-2 VC CBD O-I CP M-1 M-2 FRRD. Restaurants, sit-down/delicatessen — — — — — — — — — — — P P P P P P P C P P P.

sources.title: SEC. 31-403. - RELATION OF ZONING DISTRICTS TO COMPREHENSIVE PLAN AND PURPOSE STATEMENTS.
sources.excerpt: CBD (CENTRAL BUSINESS DISTRICT). The CBD district provides concentrated downtown civic retail, service, office, residential and other uses in the existing central business district of the City of Suffolk. Urban design standards are required in order to protect property values, promote pedestrian activity and to maintain the unique character of the center. The CBD district promotes the long-term vitality of this economic and residential mixed use center.

project_description: Build a new single-family detached dwelling with two employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation only, in the S-R Suburban Residential District, with public water and sewer service consistent with the district's permitted uses.

sources.title: B. - Permitted Uses.
sources.excerpt: 1. Single family detached dwellings, with public water and sewer service. 2. Accessory buildings and uses, limited to detached carport and garages, tool sheds, children's playhouses and play structures, doghouses, and accessory off-street parking and loading spaces. 3. Churches and places of worship. 4. Home occupations. 5. Private swimming pools and tennis courts. 6. Public parks and playgrounds. 7. Yard sale and/or garage sale (temporary). (Ord. No. 2004-02.04, Art. 3.C, § B, 2-17-04)

project_description: Open a sit-down restaurant with six employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation of an existing storefront, in the CB-C Central Business Commercial District of downtown Danville, consistent with the district's permitted restaurant use.

sources.title: B. - Permitted Uses.
sources.excerpt: Permitted uses shall be those in the following categories which do not exceed 20,000 square feet in lot coverage or a floor area ratio (FAR) of 3.0: 1. Banks and financial institutions (without drive-thru facilities). 2. Bed and breakfast, inn, or tourist home (as defined). 3. Business service and office supply establishments. 4. Call center. 5. Churches and places of worship. 6. Day-care facilities (adult or child). 7. Data storage and data processing center. 8. Health club, spa and fitness center. 9. Home occupations. 15. Offices (general and professional). 16. Patio dining. 18. Personal service establishments. 22. Residential dwellings, as an ancillary use in structures with commercial uses on the first floor, and subject to Additional Regulations pertaining to this district. 23. Restaurants. 24. Retail sales establishments.

project_description: Build a new single-family detached dwelling with two employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation only, in the SF-6 Single Family 6 district, consistent with the district's purpose of low-density single family detached dwellings.

sources.title: 3.2.12. Uses for the Residential Base Zoning District
sources.excerpt: PURPOSE The purpose of this section is to authorize the establishment and continuation of land uses that are allowed on a parcel in a Residential base zoning district. Table 3.2.12, Principal, Accessory, and Temporary Use Table for Residential Base Zoning Districts, lists allowable uses: RESIDENTIAL USES Household Living Uses: Dwelling, single-family detached P P P P P P/C P P (permitted in the SF-2, SF-4, SF-6, SF-10, and SF-T single-family districts); Dwelling, multi-family P/C P/C P/C P/C; Home occupation P/C P/C P/C P/C P/C P/C P/C P/C P/C. COMMERCIAL USES Vehicle Sales and Service Uses: Not permitted.

project_description: Open a sit-down restaurant with six employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation of an existing ground-floor space, in the D-BC Downtown Business Center district, consistent with the downtown use table's restaurant allowance.

sources.title: 3.4.11. Uses for the Downtown Base Zoning Districts
sources.excerpt: PURPOSE The purpose of this section is to authorize the establishment and continuation of land uses that are allowed on a parcel in a Downtown base zoning district. Table 3.4.11, Principal, Accessory, and Temporary Use Table for Downtown Base Zoning Districts, lists allowable uses: COMMERCIAL USES Eating and Drinking Uses: Restaurant P/C P/C P/C P/C P/C P/C (permitted in all six downtown base districts, D-W, D-BC, D-MU, D-AD, D-FN, and D-SP); Hotel or motel P P P P P P; RESIDENTIAL USES Dwelling, multi-family P P P P P P/C; Dwelling, townhouse P P P P P.

project_description: Build a new single-unit dwelling with two employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation only, in the R-8 residential zone, consistent with the zone's permitted single-unit dwelling use.

sources.title: 3-302 - Permitted uses.
sources.excerpt: The following uses are permitted in the R-8 zone: (A) Single-unit dwelling; (A.1) Two-unit dwelling; (A.2) Multi-unit dwelling up to four units; (B) Accessory uses, as permitted by section 7-100; (C) Child or elder care home, as permitted by section 7-500; (D) Church; (E) Home occupation, as permitted by section 7-300; (F) Public park; (G) Public school; (H) Utilities, as permitted by section 7-1200.

project_description: Open a business and professional office with six employees, posted operating hours, and interior renovation of an existing commercial space, in the CG commercial general zone, consistent with the zone's permitted office use.

sources.title: 4-402 - Permitted uses.
sources.excerpt: The following uses are permitted in the CG zone: (A) Single-unit dwelling; (A.1) Two-unit dwelling; (A.2) Townhouse dwelling; (B) Multi-unit dwelling; (B.1) Auxiliary dwelling, not to exceed four units; (C) Business and professional office; (D) Cemetery; (E) Church; (E.1) Day care center; (E.2) Health and athletic club or fitness studio; (F) Medical laboratory; (G) Health profession office; (I) Personal service establishment; (J) Public school; (J.1) Recreation and entertainment uses, indoor; (J.2) Restaurant.
